title: Actinocephalus compactus
intro: Actinocephalus compactus is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as a type of sedge or a small, grass-like plant. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus compactus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Actinocephalus compactus is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. This family consists of approximately 2,000 species, mainly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Actinocephalus is a part of this family and comprises a limited number of species. Actinocephalus compactus is a specific species within this genus, native to South America. The classification of Actinocephalus compactus is further specified by its binomial nomenclature, which includes its genus and species names.
